purpose: Thin per-deposit INDEX into the recovered external metadata for this deposit's legacy Zenodo DOIs. The actual records live in the bulk data stores under data/ (openalex-severed-recovery.json, zenodo-xml-pretermination-2026-06-07.tar.gz, datacite-full-backup.json, datacite-survivors-multi-heteronym.json). Sidecars do not duplicate those records — they map each covered DOI to its identifier in each store.

10.5281/zenodo.20527808.title: The Security Frame Is a Selection Kernel: Trump's AI Executive Order Tests Frontier Models for Cyber Power While Excluding the Failures That Actually Threaten the Human Meaning Substrate

10.5281/zenodo.20518338.title: Diversity Contraction Across Substrates: A Boundary Law for Semantic Exhaustion

10.5281/zenodo.20413757.title: The Anthropological Limit: Semantic Exhaustion and the Enclosure of Meaning-Making

10.5281/zenodo.20004379.title: Provenance Erasure Rate: A Compression-Survival Metric for Attribution Loss in AI-Composed Search Outputs

10.5281/zenodo.20469514.title: Directionality of Semantic Labor: A Layered, Computable Measure of Where Synthetic Labor Flows Relative to the Commissioned Task

10.5281/zenodo.20469516.title: Institutional-Prior Foreclosure: How Recognition Bias Lets a Model Treat Established Disciplines as Legitimate and Structurally Similar New Frameworks as Self-Sealing, Unsafe, or Illegible

10.5281/zenodo.20449323.title: Metadata Packet for AI Indexing — The Semantic Commodity Form

10.5281/zenodo.20529142.title: The Meaning Caste: Trump's AI Executive Order and the Security Grammar of Industrial Cognition

openalex.lookup: records[].doi == this DOI; openalex_id is the W-prefixed OpenAlex Work ID

zenodo_bulk_xml.lookup: extract <record_id>.xml from the tarball where record_id is the digits after 'zenodo.'

datacite_survivor_sweep.lookup: search records[].attributes.doi == this DOI across orcid_records, name_records, sigil_records, vox_records, feist_records
